description: A project timeline (Gantt): one GanttBar per task/activity on rows, positioned along a CONTINUOUS truncated-day date axis on cols (the timeline), sized by a duration measure (bar length) and colored by a phase/status dimension. Distinct from gantt-chart, whose schedule matrix uses DISCRETE Year/Month date parts and two DATEDIFF calcs across a start AND an end date (so it cannot bind a single-date schema); this single-start-date timeline binds the committed fixture. Factory-route template, generalized to placeholders + generic field names; structurally compiled, NOT live render-verified.
avoid_when: The ask needs a schedule MATRIX broken out by discrete Year/Month columns with a computed ship/lead lag and Late/On-Time/Early status across a start AND an end date — that is gantt-chart; this timeline plots one continuous start-date axis and a plain duration measure.

The dataset has no numeric duration/length measure to size the bars — a GanttBar with no size collapses to zero-length ticks; bind a duration (days/periods) measure to the size slot or choose a dot/strip timeline instead.

slots.notes: The activity/task/phase label on rows — one GanttBar row per member. XML column-instance [none:Task:nk].

The bar's START position on the CONTINUOUS date axis (Day-Trunc, green [tdy:Start Date:qk]) — the timeline. A single date field; only one derivation, so no qualified key is required (contrast gantt-chart's two-date shape). See hazard continuous-date-axis-required.

The bar LENGTH (task duration in days/periods) on the size encoding — SUM([Duration]) → [sum:Duration:qk]. A plain measure, NOT a DATEDIFF calc, so the template binds a single-date schema. See hazard gantt-size-generic-measure.
The phase/status dimension on color — one hue per member. XML column-instance [none:Phase:nk].

hazards.detail: Compiled HERMETICALLY via the factory route (no live Tableau Desktop). portability_evidence.render_verified='none' and fast_path_eligible=false: the template binds the committed fixture (fixture_bind computed true) but has NO live render-readback proof. A live-YYYY-MM-DD stamp is EARNED later by the golden-parity gate / a hand-stamp with render_evidence, never granted at compile time.

The GanttBar length is driven by SUM([Duration]), a plain quantitative measure, not a DATEDIFF(end,start) duration calc (contrast gantt-chart's Ship Lag). This is what lets the template bind a single-date schema, but the size slot must bind a real duration/length-in-days measure — binding an unrelated measure sizes the bars by that measure instead of task duration.

cols carries a CONTINUOUS truncated-day date [tdy:Start Date:qk] (green pill) — the timeline axis. The start_date slot must bind a real date/datetime field; binding a non-date field (or a discrete date part) collapses the timeline to categorical headers and the GanttBars lose their time positions.
